product_overview: MCPForge is a web app that converts OpenAPI specifications into production-ready MCP server projects through a visual interface. Users can upload or fetch specs, map endpoints to MCP types, configure server settings, preview generated code, and download packaged projects.
core_goals: Allow developers to convert OpenAPI specs to MCP servers without CLI tools
Provide visual control over endpoint-to-MCP-type mapping
Generate production-ready, downloadable MCP server projects
Save and manage multiple conversion projects per user

features.name: User Sign In
features.description: Authenticate existing users with email and password using credentials provider and redirect to the dashboard on success.
user_flows: Navigate to /auth/signin -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Sign In button to submit -> See loading state on button during submission -> Redirect to /dashboard

Navigate to /auth/signin -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Sign In button to submit -> See loading state on button during submission -> See error message on invalid credentials -> Stay on /auth/signin

Navigate to /auth/signin -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Press Enter key to submit from password field -> See loading state on button during submission -> Redirect to /dashboard

features.name: User Sign Up
features.description: Register new accounts with name, email, password, and confirm password, with inline validation and auto-login on success.
user_flows: Navigate to /auth/signup -> Fill optional name field -> Fill email field -> Fill password field with strength requirements -> Fill confirm password field -> Click Create Account button -> See loading state during submission -> Redirect to /dashboard
Navigate to /auth/signup -> Leave email empty -> Click Create Account button -> See inline validation error for email -> Stay on /auth/signup

Navigate to /auth/signup -> Fill email field with an already registered email -> Fill other fields correctly -> Click Create Account button -> See server error toast 'Email already registered' -> Stay on /auth/signup

features.name: User Sign Out
features.description: Sign out from any authenticated page via the navbar user dropdown which clears the session and redirects to sign in.
user_flows: On /dashboard -> Click user avatar in navbar -> Click Sign out button in dropdown -> See redirect to /auth/signin
On /dashboard -> Click user avatar in navbar -> Click Sign out button in dropdown -> Network error on signout API -> See error toast 'Failed to sign out' -> Stay on /dashboard

features.name: Route Protection
features.description: Protect authenticated routes and redirect unauthenticated users to signin; prevent auth pages from being used when already logged in.
user_flows: Access /dashboard while not authenticated -> Redirect to /auth/signin with return URL
Access /auth/signin while already authenticated -> Redirect to /dashboard
Call a protected API without authentication -> Receive 401 JSON { error: 'Unauthorized' }

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Marketing landing page with CTAs that route users to signup or signin and show app preview and features.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> View hero, feature grid, and app preview mockup -> Click Get started button -> Navigate to /auth/signup
Navigate to / -> Click Sign in link in header -> Navigate to /auth/signin
Navigate to / -> Click GitHub link -> Browser opens external repository in new tab -> External site blocked by browser -> See network or blocked notification

features.name: Dashboard Project List
features.description: Display the user's projects as cards with search, create, and delete capabilities.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> See project cards with name, spec title, endpoint count, created and updated dates -> Click a project card -> Navigate to /projects/[id]
Navigate to /dashboard -> Type in search input to filter projects by name -> See filtered project cards update in real time -> Clear search input -> See all projects restored
Navigate to /dashboard -> Click delete button on project card -> Confirm delete in AlertDialog -> See success toast 'Project deleted' -> Project card removed from list
Navigate to /dashboard -> Click delete button on project card -> Confirm delete in AlertDialog -> Backend deletion fails -> See error toast 'Failed to delete project' -> Project card remains

features.name: Create Project via File Upload
features.description: Create a new project by uploading an OpenAPI .json or .yaml file using drag-and-drop or file picker with client-side validation.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Upload File tab -> Drag and drop a .json file into the dropzone -> See filename and size displayed -> Click Create Project button -> See 'Creating...' loading state -> Redirect to /projects/[id]
Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Upload File tab -> Drag and drop a .txt file -> See error 'Only .json, .yaml, and .yml files are accepted' -> File not accepted
Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Upload File tab -> Upload a supported file over 5MB -> See error 'File must be under 5MB' -> File not accepted

features.name: Create Project via Paste
features.description: Create a new project by pasting OpenAPI JSON or YAML content into a textarea with syntax validation and character count.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Paste Content tab -> Paste valid OpenAPI JSON into textarea -> See character count update -> Click Create Project button -> See 'Creating...' loading state -> Redirect to /projects/[id]

Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Paste Content tab -> Paste invalid JSON into textarea -> Click Create Project button -> See alert 'Invalid JSON syntax: {parse error message}' -> Fix JSON and re-submit
Navigate to /projects/new -> Leave paste content empty -> Click Create Project button -> See alert 'No content provided. Please upload a file, paste content, or fetch from a URL.'

features.name: Create Project via URL Fetch
features.description: Fetch an OpenAPI spec from a remote URL through the server-side proxy, preview the fetched content, and create the project.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Fetch from URL tab -> Enter valid spec URL in input field -> Click Fetch button -> See loading state while fetching -> See 'Spec fetched successfully' and preview -> Click Create Project button -> Redirect to /projects/[id]
Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Fetch from URL tab -> Enter invalid URL format -> Click Fetch button -> See error 'Invalid URL format' -> Fix URL
Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter project name -> Click Fetch from URL tab -> Enter valid URL but remote returns 404 -> Click Fetch button -> See error 'Failed to fetch: 404' -> Retry or change URL

features.name: Spec Validation on Create
features.description: Validate uploaded/pasted/fetched spec with swagger-parser and show clear error messages or create the project and default mappings on success.
user_flows: On /projects/new with valid spec selected -> Click Create Project button -> Validate spec with swagger-parser -> Create project, parse endpoints, create default mappings, create default config -> Redirect to /projects/[id]
On /projects/new with pasted invalid YAML -> Click Create Project button -> Validate spec -> See alert 'Invalid YAML syntax: {parse error message}' -> Fix YAML and retry
On /projects/new with content missing 'paths' field -> Click Create Project button -> Validate spec -> See alert 'Specification is missing required "paths" field.' -> Fix spec

features.name: Project Detail Page (Layout & Not Found)
features.description: Project detail view shows header with editable project name, endpoints table, config panel, and tabs for Endpoints, Preview, and History. Show 404 when project doesn't exist or not owned.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] while authenticated and owner -> See header with project name and back arrow -> See two-column layout with endpoints table and server config panel -> Tabs default to 'Endpoints'
Navigate to /projects/[id] with non-existent id or not owner -> See 404 page with 'Project not found' message -> Click 'Back to Dashboard' -> Navigate to /dashboard

features.name: Endpoint Explorer Table
features.description: View detailed endpoint mappings including method badges, path, operation ID, summary, params count, and MCP type dropdown with auto-save.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> See endpoint table with method badges, path, operation ID, summary, params, and MCP Type dropdowns -> See summary bar with total endpoints and type counts
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Apply filters that yield no matching endpoints -> See empty state 'No endpoints match filters' in the table

features.name: Endpoint Search and Filtering
features.description: Filter endpoints by path or operationId, HTTP method, and MCP type with combined AND logic and pagination reset.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Type in search input to filter by path substring -> See table update to matching rows -> Clear search to restore results

Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Select HTTP method from method filter dropdown -> Select MCP type filter -> See combined filters applied (AND) -> Click clear method filter by selecting 'All Methods' -> See method filter cleared
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Enter search that matches no endpoints -> See 'No endpoints match filters' message

features.name: Endpoint Pagination
features.description: Paginate endpoint lists when more than 20 endpoints exist, with controls to navigate pages and display counts.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] with more than 20 endpoints -> See pagination controls and 'Showing 1-20 of N endpoints' -> Click next page button -> See page indicator update and table show next set of endpoints
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> On last page click next page button -> No change in page number (disabled) -> Remain on last page

features.name: Single MCP Type Change
features.description: Change the MCP type of a single endpoint from the table; changes are optimistically applied, saved via API, and rolled back on failure.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row -> Select new type 'Tool' -> See optimistic update in table -> See loading indicator during save -> See summary counts refresh after save
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click MCP type dropdown on an endpoint row -> Select new type -> Network error during save -> See error toast 'Failed to save' -> Dropdown reverts to previous value

features.name: Bulk MCP Type Actions
features.description: Select multiple endpoints and change their MCP type in bulk with confirmation of updated count and disappearing bulk bar.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Check individual endpoint checkboxes -> See bulk action bar appear -> Click 'Set as Resource' -> See success toast 'Updated X endpoints' -> Bulk action bar disappears

Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click select all checkbox to select visible rows -> Click bulk action 'Set as Tool' -> Backend bulk update fails -> See error toast 'Failed to update endpoints' -> Selected rows remain unchanged

features.name: Server Config Panel
features.description: Configure transport, auth method, server name, version, port, and base URL with auto-save and validation feedback.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In config panel select transport 'SSE' -> See port field appear -> Edit port field to 4000 -> See auto-save indicator 'Saving...' then 'Saved'
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In config panel select port and enter 999 -> See validation error 'Port must be between 1024 and 65535' -> Fix port to valid value -> See 'Saved' indicator
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Change server name -> Network error on auto-save -> See 'Failed to save' in red -> Change retries after connectivity restored -> See 'Saved'

features.name: Code Preview
features.description: Preview generated project files with syntax highlighting, multiple file tabs, copy-to-clipboard, and live regeneration on changes.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click Preview tab -> View default 'index.ts' tab with syntax highlighted code and line numbers -> Click 'package.json' tab -> See JSON highlighted
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In Preview tab click copy button on a file -> See toast 'Copied to clipboard' -> Copy button shows checkmark for 2 seconds
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Change an endpoint mapping or config -> See 'Regenerating...' overlay briefly -> Updated code appears in Preview tab
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Trigger generation but backend fails -> See error toast 'Failed to generate preview' -> Preview remains at last successful version

features.name: Download Project
features.description: Generate and download the MCP server as a zip file; warn when all endpoints are excluded.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click Download button -> See loading state with 'Generating project...' -> Browser downloads zip file named 'mcpforge-{project-name-kebab}.zip' -> See success toast

Navigate to /projects/[id] where all endpoints are 'Excluded' -> Click Download button -> See warning 'All endpoints are excluded. Your generated server will have no tools or resources.' -> Click 'Download Anyway' -> ZIP generated
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click Download button -> Generation fails server-side -> See error toast 'Failed to generate project' -> No download occurs

features.name: Version History
features.description: List generated project versions with expandable snapshots showing mapping and config at the time of generation.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click History tab -> See list of versions with number, timestamp, and summary -> Click a version to expand and view config snapshot -> Click again to collapse
Navigate to /projects/[id] with no versions -> Click History tab -> See empty state 'No versions yet. Download your project to create the first version.'

features.name: Inline Project Name Edit
features.description: Edit the project name inline on the project detail page with Enter to save, Escape to cancel, and visual save state.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click pencil icon next to project name -> Edit name in input field -> Press Enter to save -> See loading state during save -> See updated name displayed
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click pencil icon next to project name -> Edit name in input field -> Press Escape to cancel -> See original name restored
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Edit name and press Enter -> Backend save fails -> See error toast 'Failed to save project name' -> Input returns to previous value

features.name: Navbar
features.description: Top navigation with logo, dashboard link, and user menu showing session actions and sign in/up when logged out.
user_flows: From any page -> Click MCPForge logo in navbar -> Navigate to /dashboard
From non-authenticated view -> See 'Sign In' and 'Sign Up' buttons in navbar -> Click Sign Up -> Navigate to /auth/signup
From authenticated view on any page -> Click user avatar to open dropdown -> See user name and email -> Click Sign out -> Redirect to /auth/signin

tech_stack: JetBrains Mono and DM Sans fonts
